nodes.title: Note - VAE
widgets_values: The VAE (Variational Autoencoder) is responsible for encoding images into latent space (compressed representations) and then decoding them back into pixel space (actual frames).

nodes.title: Note - Base Precision
widgets_values:
fp8_fast seems to cause quality degradation

fp_16_fast enables "Full FP16 Accmumulation in FP16 GEMMs" feature available in the very latest pytorch nightly, this is around 20% speed boost. 

Sageattn if you have it installed can be used for almost double inference speed.

nodes.title: Note - WanVideo TextEncode
widgets_values: This node converts your written prompt into embeddings (math) that the Wan model can actually use to guide generation.

nodes.title: Note - Enhance A Video
widgets_values:
This node is for video generation that improves video quality by applying adjustable enhancements during the rendering process.

Best used with Unipc scheduler.

Higher values - more aggressive strengthening of features (sharpening, contrast, detail emphasis).

Lower values - gentler, subtler influence.

nodes.title: Note - WanVideo Sampler
widgets_values:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Steps - number of denoising iterations. Each step gradually removes noise from the latent representation, refining the image/video closer to your prompt and conditioning inputs.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CFG - A trick diffusion models use to follow your prompt instead of drifting into random noise

The model actually runs two predictions at each step:
One with your prompt conditioning (e.g. “a cat riding a bike”).
One with no prompt conditioning (unconditional).

Then it blends them using this formula:
Prediction = Unconditional + CFG * (Conditional - Unconditional)

If the model is NOT distilled:

Low CFG:
     - Loose guidance, more natural/organic outputs.
     - Less flicker across frames (good for stability).
     - But the prompt influence is weaker → might not look like what you asked.

Medium CFG:
     - Stronger adherence to prompt.
     - Still manageable flicker for short/mid-length clips.
     - Often the “sweet spot” for WAN text2vid.

High CFG:
     - Very literal prompt following (sharper features, strong colors).
     - But big risk: flicker, instability, or “overcooked” look.
     - Frames can diverge because the model is being forced too hard at every step.

------------------------

Distilled models (like WAN 2.1 distilled, Speed/CausVid versions, etc.) are trained to bake in guidance during training.

Using CFG > 1 doesn’t give you “more guidance” — it just amplifies noise/artifacts, because the guidance is already built into the distilled weights.


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Shift - controls the amount of noise discrepancy between frames, which directly influences the amount of movement and motion within the generated video

Low shift = less movement

High shift = more movement

Lower Values (2-5):
Smoother, more predictable motion.
Good for lower-resolution videos. 

Higher Values (6-12):
More dynamic or intense motion.
Can lead to more detail but also potentially character tearing, structural collapse, or other artifacts. 

Example:
A person throws a ball. Too low of a shift = the ball may instantly teleport through positions on the arc of its movement, as the model tries to understand how to make it go from point A to B, depicting only small 'shifts' in its movement.

If the shift is very high (6+), the ball is allowed to move more frame to frame, resulting in a more fluid movement, because its allowed to shift across it's arc.

If you set it absurdly high (30+) it will travel at the speed of light and take on the form of a new dimension (don't do this unless your going for abstract.  

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Seed - determines the initial random noise from which the AI will build your final picture

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Control After Generate - Fixed

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Overall:
steps = how many times you refine,
cfg = how strongly you push toward the prompt,
shift = when during those steps the details show up
high quality video of a soldier walking through the jungle
色调艳丽，过曝，静态，细节模糊不清，字幕，风格，作品，画作，画面，静止，整体发灰，最差质量，低质量，JPEG压缩残留，丑陋的，残缺的，多余的手指，画得不好的手部，画得不好的脸部，畸形的，毁容的，形态畸形的肢体，手指融合，静止不动的画面，杂乱的背景，三条腿，背景人很多，倒着走

nodes.title: Note - Block Swap
widgets_values:
Adjust the blocks to swap based on your VRAM, this is a tradeoff between speed and memory usage.

Transfers parts of the models to the CPU/RAM and off of the GPU. Stops the workflow from OOM (Out of Memory.) 


IMPORTANT!
When at least 1 VACE block is swapped, the intermediate outputs are also offloaded reducing VRAM usage considerably

nodes.title: Note - Text Encoder
widgets_values:
Turns your text prompts into embeddings the video model understands.

It handles natural language prompts, encodes them into vectors that Wan can condition on.

T5 encoder is basically the language brain of Wan.

nodes.title: Note - Triton
widgets_values: If you have Triton installed, connect this for ~30% speed increase
